goal: Add status to autoSyncCommands so the daily-entry-point status reflects fresh team data

summary.title: Auto-sync status (gated by freshness window) — daily entry point now reflects fresh team data
summary.what: Adds "status" to autoSyncCommands in internal/cli/root.go and rewrites the contradictory doc comment that argued both sides of the question without resolving. With rc7 having repositioned status as the daily entry point (Recent sealed intents block + Suggestions block), stale data is now functionally wrong rather than just stale. Existing safety nets unchanged: freshness window caps the fetch rate at 5 min, network failures fall through to local data with a warning, --no-sync flag is the per-call escape.
summary.why: The user surfaced the question after the rc7 status PR landed. The pre-existing doc comment internally contradicted itself: one half argued FOR auto-syncing collaboration surfaces, the other half argued AGAINST. The argument was paused at "check only" enabled. rc7 forces resolution because the new Recent sealed block IS team activity, not local state — showing it stale is the exact failure mode the redesign was meant to prevent.
summary.user_goal: Make `mainline status` produce truthful collaboration-state output without forcing the user to remember to sync first. The 5-min freshness window means it does not pay a network round-trip on every call.

decisions.point: status only vs status + context + list-proposals
decisions.rationale: The user asked specifically about status. context and list-proposals are arguably the same case but they are often called from scripts where a network round-trip is unwelcome; held back without explicit user direction. Documented as future enhancement.
Existing infrastructure. Bounds cost without changing the surface contract. A future PR can per-command tune the window if dogfood reveals 5 min is wrong for status specifically.

decisions.point: Doc comment cleanup vs leave the contradiction
decisions.chose: rewrite cleanly into Membership criterion / Notably absent
decisions.rationale: The contradiction was the reason the auto-sync question lingered. Resolving it in code without resolving it in docs would just create the same drift again. The new comment explicitly states why each non-member is held back.

rejected.alternative: Auto-sync ALL read commands (context, list-proposals, log)
rejected.reason: Out of scope for this turn. log already has its --sync opt-in flag; context and list-proposals need a separate decision based on script-usage patterns.

rejected.alternative: Add a separate config knob `[status] auto_sync = true`
rejected.reason: Per the existing comment principle: it is product behaviour, not team policy. Hardcode rather than config-knob.

rejected.alternative: Reduce the freshness window for status specifically
rejected.reason: The 5-min default already balances cost vs freshness; tuning per-command would add complexity for a benefit nobody has measured needing.

risks: Status now does a network round-trip on the first call after a 5-min idle. Marginal cost (a single fetch), but users on slow networks see a small lag they did not see before. Mitigated by maybeAutoSync printing "Syncing with team..." so the lag has visible cause.

Scripts that grep `mainline status --json` repeatedly may want to add --no-sync to keep behaviour instant. The flag is global and predates this PR; existing scripts that already used it are unaffected.
followups: Consider adding context and list-proposals to autoSyncCommands once script-usage patterns are clearer. Tracked in the comment.
If 5-min freshness is wrong for status (too aggressive or too lenient), tune Sync.FreshnessSeconds — currently global across all auto-syncing commands.

architectural_claims: `mainline status` is now in autoSyncCommands and runs sync subject to the freshness window before producing output.
autoSyncCommands doc comment is structured as Membership criterion / Notably absent — each non-member explicitly justified.
behavioral_changes: `mainline status` now syncs (subject to 5-min freshness window) before printing.
No-op when within freshness window or when --no-sync passed.
